text:
The 2026 AI content stack.

What's essential, what's optional, and what's the secret weapon.

 ESSENTIAL (everyone needs these):
- Claude or ChatGPT ($20/mo) — Scripting, ideation, strategy
- Perplexity ($20/mo) — Research and trend identification
- One video model — Kling

Happy to share that the SANS OSINT Europe Summit 2026 is coming to Amsterdam 

And the Call for Papers is now open.

On 15–16 June, OSINT investigators, analysts and researchers will come together to share:
• emerging techniques
• AI & automation
• real-world case studies

 AI Research has 21 papers accepted to ICLR 2026, advancing work across LLM reasoning, evaluation systems, knowledge graphs, and agent architectures. Our research addresses critical challenges in making AI systems more reliable, efficient, and effective for enterprise

Niels Provos (from OpenBSD's systrace) is

The full 2026 AI stack:

Research -- ChatGPT Deep Research / Consensus
Writing -- Claude
Browsing -- Perplexity + Comet
Learning -- NotebookLM
Images -- Gemini (Nano Banana Pro)
Video -- Sora 2 / Veo 3
Automation -- n8n
Coding -- Cursor
Music -- Suno
Visuals -- Napkin AI

A new 2026 Stanford review of Educational AI research highlights some potential benefits, as well as the very real risks of throwing these tools at students. 

Not surprisingly,

SeqHub AI Academy is hosting a Research Hackathon on Audio Intelligence for Long-Form Speech (March 20-21, 2026).
If you have strong ML/AI skills and are interested in applied research, this is worth exploring.

The registration link is available below this tweet.
